result.stderr:
strans 0.0.5
Copyright (C) 2019 Moritz Beller (Inventitech)
USAGE:
Standard usage where strans infers a string transformation rule from one 
example, extracting the file extension from the input on STDIN (for example, 
from ls):
  strans --after bin --before file.bin
Usage with multiple examples to infer string transformation rules stored in 
file examples. Syntax in example is one transformation example per line as 
before => after:
  strans --example-file examples

  -b, --before          Required. An example of an input line before 
                        transformation

  -a, --after           Required. An example of an output line after 
                        transformation

  -f, --example-file    Required. A file containing one or multiple 
                        transformation examples. The before and after transfer 
                        string are separated by => on the same line. One line 
                        per example.

  --save                Save the inferred program in a file, based on the 
                        examples. Does not execute the actual program.

  --load                Required. Load a previously inferred program from a 
                        file.

  --describe            Print-out a human-readable description of the inferred 
                        program, based on the examples. Do not perform any 
                        actual string transformation.

  --help                Display this help screen.

  --version             Display version information.
